title: Per-state village-boundaries index
description: Manifest sidecar that lists which per-district village-boundary files (`<S>-villages-<dist_lgd>.geojson`) exist on disk for a given state. The frontend loader reads this at startup so it can decide whether a village-level drill is fetchable for a given district WITHOUT a 404-probe (Fowler v4 nit 1, TODO/TN-GRANULAR-GEO-PLAN.md). Lives at `datasets/boundaries/in/geojson/<S>-villages-index.json`.

x-changelog.description: Initial schema. Records {state_lgd, district_lgd_codes:[...], generated_at}. Emitted by tools/boundaries/snapshot.py alongside the per-district village GeoJSONs (the source.split_by block). Pure additive — pre-existing boundary files unaffected.

Major: add required top-level `sources` array (CLAUDE.md §12). The index manifest is itself a published artifact under datasets/, so it carries its own provenance — copied at emit time from the upstream the per-district village shards were derived from. Bumped same day as v1.0; no v1.0 data files were ever committed beyond the bundle this fixup commit also corrects.

sources.description: CLAUDE.md §12 provenance — typically copied verbatim from the upstream that produced the per-district shards this index enumerates.

state_lgd.description: LGD numeric state code (e.g. "33" for Tamil Nadu).

district_lgd_codes.description: LGD numeric district codes for which a village-level boundary file exists on disk under the same directory. Sorted ascending lexicographically. Empty array = no village layer emitted yet for this state.

generated_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp when the emitter wrote this index.
